meta.title: Alexis — Junior Developer
meta.description: Junior Developer crafting structured, coherent and durable digital experiences.
meta.ogDescription: Junior Developer crafting structured, coherent and durable digital experiences.
meta.keywords: web developer, front-end developer, portfolio, react, digital interfaces, rouen, paris

about.text: Rouen — Paris. I design interfaces built to last — structure first, aesthetics second. Audiovisual sharpens my eye, code disciplines my thinking. I don't build to impress, I build to work.
about.toolsText2: React for modularity, PHP for backend stability, Git for history.
about.toolsText3: I learn what solves real problems — not to collect tools.
about.visionText1: I don't code just to tick boxes.
about.visionText2: I build interfaces shaped around the user.
about.visionText3: A good product is structure and design working together.

projects.approachText1: Every project answers a specific need. No page built just to exist — intention drives everything, from the first component to the last pixel.
projects.approachText2: I pick the stack after understanding the problem. Not before.
projects.approachText3: A good project reads itself at first glance. Architecture and interface — same standard.

project1.title: Finance Management App
project1.description:
I developed a personal finance management application to track my income, expenses, savings, and monthly budgets. The goal was to create a clear and structured tool that helps visualize spending habits and better understand overall financial balance.

Built entirely with PHP, SQL, JS, and Tailwind for styling, the application includes custom categories, a light/dark mode, and a complete operation management system. Each new transaction automatically updates totals and the remaining balance.

The interface also allows comparing two different months to identify variations in spending and savings. This solo project allowed me to design a truly useful tool while implementing real business logic and a functional, readable interface.

project2.title: ShiftUp
project2.description:
ShiftUp is a gamified CSR application designed for companies.

As the project manager within a team, I created the site architecture, produced the Figma wireframes and mockups, developed the showcase website, handled the front-end integration in React/Tailwind, and contributed to the PHP backend and database.

I also worked on the site's SEO. The application includes a challenge system, a KPI dashboard, and tools to track the evolution of responsible actions.

project3.title: Weather Dashboard
project3.description:
As part of the SAE 303 project, I developed a weather dashboard to analyze climate data from the Marne region (1950–2022). The project relied on a dedicated database and statistical calculations to highlight climate trends.

Built entirely solo, it combines PHP, SQL, custom CSS, Chart.js, and Leaflet to produce interactive visualizations such as trend curves, year‑to‑year comparisons, histograms, and mapping.

The interface allows filtering data by time range or weather station to observe the impact of climate change through dynamic charts and an interactive map.

project4.title: Seconde Chance
project4.description:
Seconde Chance is a short film created as a team during my first year for the Green Awards, where we won the youth category.

My role was mainly technical: assisting with shots, managing equipment, and contributing occasionally to the ideation.

The film highlights second-hand consumption and recycling as an alternative to fast fashion, encouraging more responsible habits.
